verdict.answer_first: B states the answer cleanly in sentence 1: "POST is triggering a CORS preflight and your GET isn't"; A also answers first but adds slightly more framing.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to the core cause and fix without over-structuring: "the fix is on the server: make it respond to the OPTIONS request"; A is also decisive but longer.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use cautious language like "almost certainly" and do not invent the user's framework or exact headers.
verdict.actionability: A is slightly easier to act on because it gives a concrete header block and a useful diagnostic: "change the POST to send Content-Type: text/plain... confirms it was the preflight."
